Operation 'createBackup' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is identical to summary and provides no actionable information. Enhanced version clarifies the operation's purpose, specifies required inputs (database ID), explains the return value (Backup object with specific fields), describes side effects (backup initiation and storage), and provides context about use cases. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new backup of the specified database instance. The backup captures the current state of the database and is stored in the Databasus backup system. Requires the database ID as a path parameter. Returns a Backup object containing the backup ID, creation timestamp, size, and status. The backup process is initiated immediately and may take time to complete depending on database size. Backups can be used for disaster recovery or point-in-time restoration.
data.reasoning: Original description is identical to summary and provides no actionable information. Enhanced version clarifies the operation's purpose, specifies required inputs (database ID), explains the return value (Backup object with specific fields), describes side effects (backup initiation and storage), and provides context about use cases. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'restoreBackup' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description lacks all essential details. Enhanced version specifies required parameters (database ID and backup ID), explains the destructive nature of the operation, clarifies the return value (Database object), describes side effects (data overwrite and potential data loss), and provides important warnings about data preservation. This enables AI agents to understand the operation's impact and use it appropriately.

data.description_suggestion: Restores a database instance to a previous state using a specified backup. Requires the database ID and backup ID as path parameters. This operation overwrites the current database with the backup data, effectively rolling back to the backup's point-in-time. Returns the updated Database object reflecting the restored state. This is a destructive operation - current data not in the backup will be lost. The restoration process may take time depending on backup size. Consider creating a backup before restoration if data preservation is needed.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks all essential details. Enhanced version specifies required parameters (database ID and backup ID), explains the destructive nature of the operation, clarifies the return value (Database object), describes side effects (data overwrite and potential data loss), and provides important warnings about data preservation. This enables AI agents to understand the operation's impact and use it appropriately.

message: Operation 'executeQuery' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is too generic and provides no implementation details. Enhanced version specifies required inputs (database ID and SQL query), clarifies supported query types, explains the return value structure (QueryResult with status, rows, affected count, errors), and provides operational context (synchronous execution, timeouts, result limits). data.description_suggestion: Executes a SQL query against the specified database instance. Requires the database ID as a path parameter and the SQL query string in the request body. Supports SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations. Returns a QueryResult object containing the query execution status, result rows (for SELECT queries), affected row count (for DML operations), and any error messages if execution fails. Query execution is synchronous with a timeout limit. Results are limited to the configured maximum result set size.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic and provides no implementation details. Enhanced version specifies required inputs (database ID and SQL query), clarifies supported query types, explains the return value structure (QueryResult with status, rows, affected count, errors), and provides operational context (synchronous execution, timeouts, result limits). This helps AI agents understand what queries are supported and how to interpret results.

This operation overwrites the current database state with the backed-up data, effectively rolling back to the point when the backup was created. Returns the updated Database object reflecting the restored state. This is a destructive operation - the current database state will be replaced. Restoration time depends on backup size and database complexity. Use with caution in production environments.
